pullRequest.title: [feaLib] Raise an error for rsub with NULL target
pullRequest.body:
Glyph deletion can’t be supported with reverse chaining substitution and we were silently converting it to chaining substitution.

See discussion in https://github.com/fonttools/fonttools/issues/2952.

* Fixes https://github.com/fonttools/fonttools/issues/2952

pullRequest.title: Improve {v,h}mtx error when not enough data to decompile sidebearings
pullRequest.body:
Fixes #3843 

Sometimes hmtx or vmtx may appear truncated with not enough data to decompile the list of sidebearings at the end of the table. Currently an IndexError would be thrown which is confusing.

```
ERROR: An exception occurred during the decompilation of the 'vmtx' table
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "fontTools/ttLib/ttFont.py", line 468, in _readTable
    table.decompile(data, self)
  File "fontTools/ttLib/tables/_h_m_t_x.py", line 67, in decompile
    self.metrics[glyphName] = (lastAdvance, sideBearings[i])
                                            ~~~~~~~~~~~~^^^
IndexError: array index out of range
Dumping 'vmtx' table...
```

This PR makes it so we raise the same TTLibError(f"not enough {tableTag} data") that we already raise when we don't have enough table data to decompile the "long" metrics.

pullRequest.title: [featureVars] set NULL offset for empty ConditionSet
pullRequest.body: Fixes #3844

pullRequest.title: [feaLib] Add promoted single substitutions to aalt feature
pullRequest.body:
If a single substitution was promoted to a multiple or ligature substitutions, we would no longer add it to aalt feature (when requested) since only single and alternate substations are added there.

This can be considered a regression from lookup promotion. Since a single-looking substitution wouldn’t occurs in ligature or multiple substitution unless we promoted it, it should be safe to add these to allt feature.

Fixes https://github.com/fonttools/fonttools/issues/3845

pullRequest.title: [feaLib] Improve single substitution promotion
pullRequest.body:
Move the logic down into the builder so that it is applied after lookups are split due to e.g. different lookup flags or interspersed contextual substitution rules. Arguably the builder was the right place for this all along, but I took the either route (twice!) before.

Fixes https://github.com/fonttools/fonttools/issues/3846

pullRequest.title: fix iterable check for Python 3.13.4 and newer
pullRequest.body:
Fix the `feaLib/ast.py` snippet used to check whether a type is iterable to work correctly with Python 3.13.4.  The snippet wrongly assumed that a generator expression will raise immediately when the RHS of `in` is not iterable.  This is no longer the case with Python 3.13.4, and such a generator only raises when you actually start iterating. Use a plain `for` expression to start iterating and catch the problem more reliably.

Fixes #3854

pullRequest.title: [feaLib] Don't try to combine ligature & multisub rules
pullRequest.body:
I'm not sure that this patch is the correct solution to this problem, but it is _a_ solution.

Specifically the problem is that the AnySubstBuilder can generate multiple lookups, but when this happens those extra lookups do not get added to the appropriate feature. My solution here is to force a new lookup whenever we see that the AnySubstBuilder would otherwise contain mixed multiple/ligature sub rules, which cannot be combined anyway.

Open to alternatives, but this does fix the issue.


- closes #3866

pullRequest.title: [subset] MarkBasePos: remove empty BaseRecord
pullRequest.body: Try to fix #3892

pullRequest.title: [feaLib] allow float values in VariableScalar's axis locations
pullRequest.body: Fixes #3906  and https://github.com/googlefonts/glyphsLib/issues/1105

pullRequest.title: Don’t use deprecated uharfbuzz API
pullRequest.body:
Depend on a recent enough uharfbuzz to be able to unconditionally use serialize_with_tag(), and drop the code that tries to use repack() if repack_with_tag() is missing.

Using ancient versions of uharfbuzz is not a good idea anyway, as this misses later fixes to the repacking code.

Fixes https://github.com/fonttools/fonttools/issues/3917

pullRequest.title: Some new `varLib.avar` modules / tools
pullRequest.body:
Fixes https://github.com/fonttools/fonttools/issues/3920

New module structure is:
* `varLib.avar.build` module to build `avar` (and a missing `fvar`) binaries into a possibly empty `TTFont`,
* `varLib.avar.unbuild` module to print a `.designspace` snippet that would generate the same `avar` binary,
* `varLib.avar.map` module to take `TTFont` and do the mapping, in user/normalized space,
* `varLib.avar.plan` module moved from `avarPlanner`.

EDIT(anthrotype): The bare `fonttools varLib.avar` script is deprecated, in favour of `fonttools varLib.avar.build` or `unbuild`

pullRequest.title: [otlLib] buildCoverage: return empty Coverage instead of None
pullRequest.body:
Per the OpenType spec, coverage offsets that are not explicitly documented as nullable should not be NULL.

Here I changed otlLib.builder.buildCoverage() to always return a Coverage object (with empty glyphs list if no glyphs supplied) instead of returning None.

This fixes null offsets being written in GDEF MarkGlyphSetsDef coverage arrays, and everywhere else buildCoverage is used.

The only nullable Coverage in MATH table is already handled specially, see: https://github.com/fonttools/fonttools/issues/4003#issuecomment-3645992252

Fixes #4003

pullRequest.title: [feaLib] write 0xFFFF instead of '0' for missing nameIDs
pullRequest.body:
Specifically in FeatureParams for the cv features; the spec is poorly worded but a 0 value here may be misinterpreted by clients.

- fixes #4010

pullRequest.title: Fix SegmentToPointPen edge case
pullRequest.body:
This fixes #4014 

Fix SegmentToPointPen pen edge case: only remove a duplicate final point during closePath() if it is an on-curve point.

pullRequest.title: [feaLib] Merge chained multi subst rules with same context
pullRequest.body:
Fixes #4016.

`MultipleSubstStatement.build()` expands glyph classes by calling `add_multi_subst_chained_()` once per glyph. Each call appended a separate `ChainContextualRule`, producing one Format 3 subtable per glyph — even when
all glyphs share the same backtrack/lookahead context and lookup.

For example:

```fea
sub [A B] [c d] [a b]' by NULL;
```

... was generating 2 ChainContextSubst subtables (one for `a` and one for `b`).

Now, instead of creating a new rule each time, before appending a new rule, we check if the last rule has the same prefix, suffix and lookup, and if so we merge the glyph into the existing rule. 

The example above produces 1 subtable with both glyphs in the InputCoverage (see `chain_context_multi_subst_class.fea` test case), and I also verified the GSUB is structurally identical to the fea-rs output on the same input.

For reference, fea-rs avoids this entirely by building the context rule with the full glyph class in one step rather than per-glyph: https://github.com/googlefonts/fontc/blob/fca9914eca7c/fea-rs/src/compile/compile_ctx.rs#L891-L919

(Regarding the test `contextual_inline_multi_sub_format_2` that changed output: merging the rules made Format 3 with 2 subtables cheaper than Format 2 with 1 subtable so the format selection flipped, but the overall GSUB table shrank a few bytes so it's good).

pullRequest.title: [ttFont::saveXML] Unbreak ttx
pullRequest.body: Fixes https://github.com/fonttools/fonttools/issues/4025

pullRequest.title: Add saveXML regression tests and fix diff table filtering
pullRequest.body:
- Add regression tests for `saveXML` table/skipTables filtering that would have caught #4025
- Clarify `saveXML` docstring: both `None` and `[]` mean "dump all tables" (longstanding behavior in ttx)
- Change `fontTools.diff` argparse `nargs` from `"*"` to `"+"` for `-t`/`-x` so the CLI rejects empty table lists
- Switch `_iter_filtered_table_tags` from `is not None` to truthiness checks so `[]` and `None` are treated the same

The immediate regression was already fixed by the revert (#4026). This PR simply adds the missing test coverage and prevents the `diff` module to hit the same `[]` vs `None` ambiguity.

Closes #4025

pullRequest.title: [setup.py] Link libm for Cython extensions using math functions (#4028)
pullRequest.body:
On some Linux systems (e.g., Amazon Linux 2023 with aarch64 and Clang), math functions like sqrt, pow, fabs require explicit linkage to libm. Add libraries=["m"] to the four affected extensions on POSIX systems:
- fontTools.cu2qu.cu2qu
- fontTools.qu2cu.qu2cu
- fontTools.misc.bezierTools
- fontTools.pens.momentsPen

Fixes #4028

pullRequest.title: [glifLib] Fix regression reading glyph outlines with glyphObject=None
pullRequest.body:
Test that pointPen still receives drawing commands when reading a glyph with glyphObject=None. This is a valid use case for drawing outlines without populating glyph attributes.

Reproduces and fixes #4030

pullRequest.title: [cmap] Fix subtable sorting crash and validate unique encoding records
pullRequest.body:
- Remove `__dict__` from `CmapSubtable.__lt__()` comparison tuples — this was a Python 2 leftover that raises `TypeError` in Python 3 when two subtables share the same `(platformID, platEncID, language)`
- Add compile-time validation that each `(platformID, platEncID, language)` combination is unique, as [required by the OpenType spec](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/typography/opentype/spec/cmap#encoding-records-and-encodings):

> Each platform ID, platform-specific encoding ID, and subtable language combination may appear only once in the 'cmap' table.

Fixes #4035

pullRequest.title: [cffLib] Don't use eval()
pullRequest.body: Fixes https://github.com/fonttools/fonttools/issues/4039

pullRequest.title: [svgLib] Skip non-element nodes (comments, PIs) when drawing SVG paths
pullRequest.body:
When iterating over SVG elements, XML comment and processing instruction nodes have a non-string tag (a callable), which caused a TypeError in _strip_xml_ns(). Skip these nodes early in add_path_from_element().

Fixes #4042
